topics: personalization and user modeling for agents
open-source/open-weights model architectures and training protocols
extra_instructions: Priority tiers: PRIMARY (weight heavily) — agentic memory, personalization/user-modeling for agents, agentic design; RL post-training for LMs (esp. significant open-source/open-weights architecture or training-protocol advances and genuinely new post-training methods); LLM architecture design (esp. small-but-capable models); agent evaluation; mechanistic interpretability and AI safety. SECONDARY (include only for major advances) — world models, VLM/VLA + sim2real, robotics data/hardware. For RL/post-training and architecture, favor real methodological novelty and open-weights releases over incremental benchmark bumps or minor training-protocol tweaks. ALWAYS include a 'Didn't quite make the cut' section after the main sections: 3-5 near-miss papers that were relevant but fell just below the novelty/selection bar. One line each: link, a few words on what it is, and why it was borderline (e.g. 'incremental', 'strong but off-topic', 'interesting but thin eval') — designed to be skimmable blurbs. These are NOT counted toward the target paper count and should NOT be marked seen, so they can resurface if they gain traction. This is a permanent feature (also lets the user recalibrate the bar by replying which near-misses should have been included).
